events.description: The Great Pyramid of Giza was completed
The Code of Hammurabi was written in Babylon
The city of Carthage was founded by Phoenician settlers
The first Olympic Games were held in Greece
The Persian Wars began with the Ionian Revolt
Alexander the Great conquered the Persian Empire
Julius Caesar was assassinated in the Roman Senate
The Roman Colosseum was completed and opened
The Western Roman Empire fell to Germanic invaders
The Prophet Muhammad began receiving revelations, founding Islam
Charlemagne was crowned Emperor of the Romans by the Pope
Viking explorer Leif Erikson reached North America
The First Crusade was launched to recapture Jerusalem
King John of England signed the Magna Carta
The Black Death arrived in Europe via Sicilian ports
Joan of Arc led French forces to victory at Orléans
Constantinople fell to the Ottoman Empire under Mehmed II
Johannes Gutenberg printed the first Bible using movable type
Nicolaus Copernicus published his heliocentric model of the solar system
Isaac Newton published his laws of motion and universal gravitation
Edward Jenner introduced the first successful smallpox vaccine
Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species
Marie Curie became the first person to win two Nobel Prizes
Albert Einstein published his general theory of relativity
Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in his laboratory
Watson and Crick described the double helix structure of DNA
The first successful cloning of a mammal, Dolly the sheep, was announced
The United States dropped atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki
NATO was founded as a collective defense alliance in the West
The Korean War began when North Korea invaded South Korea
Sputnik 1 became the first artificial satellite launched into orbit
The Berlin Wall was constructed dividing East and West Germany
The Cuban Missile Crisis brought the world to the brink of nuclear war
Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon
The Berlin Wall fell, symbolizing the end of the Cold War
The Soviet Union formally dissolved into fifteen independent republics
James Watt patented an improved steam engine, powering the Industrial Revolution
Samuel Morse sent the first long-distance telegraph message
Alexander Graham Bell made the first successful telephone call
Thomas Edison demonstrated his practical incandescent light bulb
The Wright Brothers achieved the first powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk
John Logie Baird gave the first public demonstration of television
The first general-purpose electronic computer, ENIAC, was unveiled
Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN
Apple released the first iPhone, transforming mobile communication
